params.name: Poppy Bead Gold Necklace
params.description: This eye-catching necklace is crafted by meticulously welding metal wire rings of various diameters into cornflower and bead shapes, an early example of filigree technique. It was discovered in the famous Valley of the Kings, and archaeologists speculate that it belonged to Pharaoh Tawosret. Tawosret was not only the wife of Pharaoh Seti II but also served as regent for her son Seti III after Seti II's death, handling state affairs on his behalf. In the late 19th dynasty, Tawosret ruled Egypt in …
